title: Cincinnati Crime, 1999–2026 — The Long Slide, Mapped Across 50 Neighborhoods
titleOptions: Cincinnati Crime Fell 45% From Its 2002 Peak — Then the Count Changed
Which Cincinnati Neighborhood Reports the Fewest Crimes? 27 Years of Data, Mapped
description:
Twenty-seven years of reported crime in Cincinnati — a 45% fall from the 2002 peak to 2019 under the FBI's measure, then the city's own incident record, roughly level since 2020, mapped across all 50 neighborhoods.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. Every figure comes straight from the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting program and the City of Cincinnati's own open crime data. Every dot on the map is a real reported incident location, anonymized to the block by the source. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen.

The FBI's classic 'index' crimes (through 2019) and CPD's STARS incident records (2020+) are different measures — shown as two chapters, never merged into one line. CPD changed records systems in June 2024; the two published datasets are deduplicated across the transition. The city's crime datasets carry no explicit license — used with attribution to the City of Cincinnati / Cincinnati Police Department.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — the long slide
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc
2:30  The last five years — up close
2:43  Chapter 2 — the map, month by month
4:52  The busiest & safest areas
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/cincinnati-oh

Data: City of Cincinnati / Cincinnati Police Department via data.cincinnati-oh.gov (no license specified; used with attribution). Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI). Basemap © OpenStreetMap contributors.

If this was worth a look, please Like, Subscribe, and Share.
